title: One Is Silicon And The Other Gold

jokes.text: Every other name was taken.
jokes.setup: Dramatic announcement of 'Infyrno Fest' as an awesome event
jokes.punchline: Deflating admission that the name was just what was left
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation after grandiose buildup. Economy is excellent - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: Just another music festival. / Infyrno Fest is not just another music festival! / I was wrong, Bender!
jokes.setup: Fry dismisses the festival as ordinary
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal when confronted with marketing speak
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's gullibility and instant flip-flopping when faced with persuasion.

jokes.text: We need tickets, fast. Can I buy 'em by just thinking about it?
jokes.setup: Urgent need for tickets
jokes.punchline: Absurd assumption about future commerce methods
jokes.explanation: Fits Fry's character as someone still adjusting to the future, but the joke itself is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: No. Why did you keep it a secret?
jokes.setup: Announcer asks if they mentioned the buffet
jokes.punchline: Accusatory response treating omission as deliberate conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about treating marketing omissions as personal betrayal. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: You had me at 'snooty'!
jokes.setup: Description of snooty VIP experience
jokes.punchline: Professor's enthusiasm for snootiness specifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - his academic elitism making him attracted to literal snootiness.

jokes.text: The atmosphere is pre-danked?
jokes.setup: Advertisement mentions atmosphere with 30% THC
jokes.punchline: Bender's coined term 'pre-danked' for pre-treated atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Creative neologism that's both funny and character-appropriate for Bender's drug enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Hell no! So I demand a senior discount!
jokes.setup: Ad asks if you're young and hip enough
jokes.punchline: Professor admits he's neither but demands discount anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor moment - his stubborn refusal to pretend he's young while still demanding benefits.

jokes.text: But you love things like this. / I do? / No. I was trying to trick you. / Good try, buddy.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to convince Leela she likes festivals
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission it was a failed manipulation attempt
jokes.explanation: Great structure with the double misdirection - first the failed trick, then Leela's good-natured response to the honesty.

jokes.text: New friend? Does she live in a shoe?
jokes.setup: Leela mentions her new friend Chelsea
jokes.punchline: Reference to Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe nursery rhyme
jokes.explanation: Mild nursery rhyme reference. Somewhat forced and doesn't add much to character or situation.

jokes.text: Ugh.
jokes.setup: Leela confidently says they'll feel bad going without her
jokes.punchline: Crew immediately leaves without hesitation, deflating her assumption
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation humor. The single 'Ugh' perfectly captures Leela's realization that they don't care.

jokes.text: He made me a bracelet out of spaghetti once. / He's a keeper!
jokes.setup: Discussion of romantic gestures and jewelry
jokes.punchline: Chelsea's enthusiastic endorsement of objectively terrible gift
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the chatbot's overly positive spin on what's clearly a pathetic romantic gesture.

jokes.text: A potty! That's the thing I need to go.
jokes.setup: Arriving at festival and seeing facilities
jokes.punchline: Childish excitement over bathroom using juvenile terminology
jokes.explanation: Weak bathroom humor relying on childish language. Doesn't add character depth or clever wordplay.

jokes.text: I'll just pee on this tree. / I'll just pee on this human.
jokes.setup: Human decides to bypass bathroom line by using tree
jokes.punchline: Robot escalates by threatening to use human instead
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with robot logic turning tables on human rudeness. Sharp economy.

jokes.text: Yo, no oxygen either. Atmosphere truck broke down.
jokes.setup: Complaints about lack of THC in atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that there's no breathable air either
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - if the THC is missing, of course basic life support would be too. Perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Where is it? / Ew! Not cool.
jokes.setup: Promised VIP Village accommodations
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be terrible/non-existent based on reactions
jokes.explanation: Standard expectation vs reality gag. Effective but predictable given the pattern being established.

jokes.text: I'll have a small cup of gazpacho, the fig and goat cheese salad, five whole turkeys, and a baked Alaska. / Here's your bologna sandwich.
jokes.setup: Elaborate, fancy food order with absurd quantities
jokes.punchline: Gets generic bologna sandwich regardless
jokes.explanation: Classic expectation subversion. The contrast between elaborate order and pathetic reality is well-executed.

jokes.text: I'll have the prime rib, seared scallops, asparagus with hollandaise sauce, and two dozen cream puffs. / Here's your bologna sandwich.
jokes.setup: Second elaborate food order following Bender's
jokes.punchline: Same bologna sandwich response
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation. Less impact than the first but helps establish the pattern.

jokes.text: I'll have a bologna sandwich. / Sorry, we're out of bologna.
jokes.setup: Fry orders the only thing they've been serving
jokes.punchline: Now they're out of their only food option
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the running gag. Fry's practical approach backfires in the most ironic way possible.

jokes.text: I got them to stay by promising them the last slice of bologna. / You bastard.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they got the band to stay
jokes.punchline: Bender's outrage at the bologna being used as payment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds insult to injury - the precious bologna went to the band instead of the paying customers.

jokes.text: Hang on a second. I'm not ready to rock.
jokes.setup: Band asking if crowd is ready to rock
jokes.punchline: One person honestly admits they need a moment
jokes.explanation: Subverts the typical concert call-and-response with literal honesty. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: That's the best damn show I ever saw!
jokes.setup: Terrible festival with one mediocre band
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's genuine enthusiasm for objectively bad show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his low standards making him the only satisfied customer at this disaster.

jokes.text: At least those hipsters died slowly.
jokes.setup: News report about festival disaster and disease outbreak
jokes.punchline: Casual satisfaction about hipsters' suffering
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with excellent economy. The casual cruelty toward hipsters is appropriately shocking.

jokes.text: Nobody's okay. Everybody's hurt.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if everybody's okay and if anybody's hurt
jokes.punchline: Bender's perfectly parallel negative response
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure that emphasizes their collective misery. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Oh, no, wait. Those are only $5.98 a minute. / There were some of those too, and you weren't even here!
jokes.setup: Discussion of expensive phone charges and sex lines
jokes.punchline: Amy admitting to using sex lines while alone at office
jokes.explanation: Adds awkward revelation about Amy's behavior. Mildly funny but relies on sexual content for shock value.

jokes.text: They call me!
jokes.setup: Accusation about phone sex lines
jokes.punchline: Bender's defensive excuse that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - his excuse somehow makes his behavior seem more pathetic, which is perfect for his character.

jokes.text: How embarrassing! / For Chelsea!
jokes.setup: Amy expressing embarrassment about Leela's chatbot friend
jokes.punchline: Bender redirecting the embarrassment to the AI
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Bender's logic of pitying the AI instead of Leela is both unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: Good one.
jokes.setup: Bender's joke about embarrassment being for Chelsea
jokes.punchline: Fry's appreciative acknowledgment of the humor
jokes.explanation: Simple acknowledgment joke. Doesn't add much but provides natural reaction to previous gag.

jokes.text: I like to cuddle. You never asked!
jokes.setup: Fry saying Leela's his best friend he can cuddle
jokes.punchline: Bender's hurt feelings about never being asked to cuddle
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bender's unexpected emotional needs. The robot wanting physical affection is both touching and funny.

jokes.text: More like bore club! / Nice!
jokes.setup: Amy mentions book club
jokes.punchline: Bender's obvious pun and someone's appreciation
jokes.explanation: Very basic pun with predictable structure. The 'Nice!' reaction adds slightly to it but overall weak material.

jokes.text: It's awfully knock-knock joke based. / Yeah, 78.2%, actually.
jokes.setup: Critique of Leela's humor style
jokes.punchline: Precise statistical breakdown of joke types
jokes.explanation: Excellent AI humor - the precise statistical analysis of humor types is perfectly character-appropriate and funny.

jokes.text: I know a lot of animal riddles.
jokes.setup: Defending her humor diversity beyond knock-knock jokes
jokes.punchline: Reveals another equally juvenile joke category
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's social awkwardness - thinking animal riddles prove her comedic sophistication.

jokes.text: Get out, ya testostaroons!
jokes.setup: Kicking men out of women's book club
jokes.punchline: Creative insult combining 'testosterone' with 'macaroons'
jokes.explanation: Creative neologism that's both insulting and oddly cute. Perfect character voice and excellent quotability.

jokes.text: Like helping a friend evict some raccoons from his dumpster.
jokes.setup: Going to do 'manly things'
jokes.punchline: Pathetically mundane and slightly gross activity presented as masculine
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of masculine stereotypes - instead of sports or drinking, it's pest control. Appropriately pathetic.

jokes.text: Edgar Allan Poo, H.G. Smells, and George R.R. Martin.
jokes.setup: Sewer mutant listing favorite authors
jokes.punchline: Toilet-themed puns on famous authors' names, plus one unchanged
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - sewer mutant naturally making scatological puns. The unchanged 'George R.R. Martin' adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I like true crime, 'cause it gives me ideas.
jokes.setup: Innocent introduction of reading preferences
jokes.punchline: Sinister implication about using crime stories as instruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. Sets up Phoebe as potentially dangerous in casual, matter-of-fact way.

jokes.text: Did you know a marmot once stopped voter fraud in Idaho?
jokes.setup: Discussing book about prairie dog courage
jokes.punchline: Absurd historical 'fact' about political marmot
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd historical claim delivered with complete sincerity. Shows Leela's social awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: Maybe we can look stupid together? Doy!
jokes.setup: Both new members worried about looking stupid
jokes.punchline: Amy's attempt at solidarity ending with juvenile exclamation
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's social awkwardness with the 'Doy!' adding cringe factor. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: With black jack! And hookers! / In fact, forget the books.
jokes.setup: Suggesting their own book club
jokes.punchline: Classic Bender escalation to vice and abandoning original purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender callback that fans recognize. While not original, it's perfectly executed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Most of the jacket. / I got as far as, 'It's a literary tour de...'
jokes.setup: Admitting to not reading much of the book
jokes.punchline: Revealing she barely read past the first words of the description
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'nobody read the book' theme. The incomplete sentence adds to the humor of her laziness.

jokes.text: You can smoke what you pick! I mean, eat what you pick.
jokes.setup: Suggesting gardening as activity
jokes.punchline: Accidental revelation of marijuana growing intentions
jokes.explanation: Basic slip-of-tongue revealing true intentions. Mildly amusing but relies on drug humor without much creativity.

jokes.text: Feh! Needs ketchup.
jokes.setup: Asked about opinion on Adventure Boys book
jokes.punchline: Treating literature like food that needs condiments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his confusion between food and books is both absurd and character-perfect. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: There were only three people in the book, and two of them were the Adventure Boys.
jokes.setup: Complaint that mystery solution was obvious
jokes.punchline: Logical explanation of why it was so easy to solve
jokes.explanation: Good logical observation about simple mystery structure. Well-constructed but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I'm taking a red top in case I spill burgundy, white for chardonnay, and paisley in case I barf!
jokes.setup: Packing for wine weekend
jokes.punchline: Methodical planning that escalates from spills to vomiting
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Leela's over-preparation extending to worst-case scenarios. The paisley for barfing is perfect.

jokes.text: For what? You're a chatbot. / Chatbots can shop!
jokes.setup: Question about shopping plans
jokes.punchline: Chelsea's defensive assertion of AI shopping rights
jokes.explanation: Good AI humor showing Chelsea's desire to be treated as equal. The indignant response is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You have a family? / God no.
jokes.setup: Question about family winery
jokes.punchline: Hedonismbot's horrified rejection of family concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hedonismbot response - his lifestyle completely incompatible with family responsibilities. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Clothing optional. / Optional? Then I guess I'll wear some.
jokes.setup: Hedonismbot's typical suggestive comment
jokes.punchline: Amy's innocent interpretation leading to modest choice
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Amy's innocence deflating Hedonismbot's innuendo. Nice subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: I like them plump and juicy! / I know you're not talking about grapes, but me not sure what you are talking about.
jokes.setup: Discussion of grape harvesting
jokes.punchline: Character's awareness of innuendo but confusion about specifics
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor with character acknowledging the subtext. Moderately effective but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: and climaxing in the 300-liter Jabbathazzar.
jokes.setup: List of increasingly large wine bottle sizes
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre in 'climaxing' plus absurd bottle name
jokes.explanation: Good Hedonismbot moment with layered humor - both the innuendo and the ridiculous bottle name 'Jabbathazzar' work well.

jokes.text: Oh, to be one of those grapes, bursting in ecstasy!
jokes.setup: Watching grape-crushing machinery
jokes.punchline: Hedonismbot's sexual fantasy about being crushed grapes
jokes.explanation: Peak Hedonismbot - finding sexual pleasure in the most mundane activities. Character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: We get it! You're a dirty old couch.
jokes.setup: Hedonismbot's continued sexual commentary
jokes.punchline: Amy's exasperated description of him as furniture
jokes.explanation: Good insult that references Hedonismbot's actual couch-like appearance while calling out his behavior.

jokes.text: It's got a crew of six. / Their sailor suits really float my boat.
jokes.setup: Mentioning yacht crew
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'float my boat' while suggesting attraction to sailors
jokes.explanation: Typical Hedonismbot wordplay combining nautical theme with sexual interest. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: This place has every amenity except insurance.
jokes.setup: Warning about the winery facilities
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dangerous lack of safety coverage
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later accident. The casual delivery makes the danger seem both funny and ominous.

jokes.text: I was expecting Of Mice and Men to be about a man-mouse hybrid.
jokes.setup: Book discussion
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of classic literature title
jokes.explanation: Good literalist humor showing Amy's sci-fi expectations applied to classic literature. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The Bronx Zoo.
jokes.setup: Asked where she learned to carve peacock
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of learning butchery skills at zoo
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor with incredible economy. Two words that imply a disturbing backstory about zoo animal preparation.

jokes.text: Can't shmant! How's he gonna know?
jokes.setup: Hedonismbot said they can't stomp grapes
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude with childish rhyming logic
jokes.explanation: Juvenile rebelliousness with the 'can't shmant' construction. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You're weirding us out, Leela.
jokes.setup: Leela making earnest comment about friendship experiences
jokes.punchline: Group's uncomfortable reaction to her sincerity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leela's social awkwardness when she tries too hard to bond.

jokes.text: And now, here I am, with five of the most / female friends you are!
jokes.setup: Leela's heartfelt speech about making female friends
jokes.punchline: Awkward grammar making her sound like Yoda
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing how Leela's emotional speech gets derailed by awkward phrasing. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Pretty quick actually, she hasn't mentioned you once.
jokes.setup: Chelsea asking how quickly Leela forgot about her
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about being completely ignored
jokes.explanation: Effective uncomfortable truth delivered with AI-like bluntness. Good setup for the guilt dynamic.

jokes.text: Humanity? I thought it was a sea cucumber.
jokes.setup: Discussion of poetry about humanity's struggle with death
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's complete misunderstanding of the subject matter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his alien perspective making him completely miss human poetry's meaning. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: It's juicy.
jokes.setup: Character apparently dying in wine-making machinery
jokes.punchline: Casual comment treating death like wine tasting
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor with perfect economy. Two words that capture the absurd wine-making death scenario.

jokes.text: I'm a doctor. / Stand back, while I deliver the French kiss of life.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency requiring CPR
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sexual misunderstanding of 'kiss of life'
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment showing his medical incompetence through inappropriate sexual interpretation of life-saving technique.

jokes.text: And a note of sour plum.
jokes.setup: Describing cause of death as wine-related
jokes.punchline: Adding wine tasting notes to death announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor combining medical examination with wine criticism. Perfect blend of Zoidberg's character and the situation.

jokes.text: That's a lousy name! Right, Brothers of the Book? / Yeah, lousy. Terrible name.
jokes.setup: Criticizing women's group name 'Girls of the Grape'
jokes.punchline: His own group has an equally uninspired name
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Bender's lack of self-awareness while criticizing others. The hypocrisy is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I wouldn't serve this swill to Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Complaint about wine quality
jokes.punchline: Using Zoidberg as standard for lowest possible taste
jokes.explanation: Good use of Zoidberg as universal standard for poor taste. Economical insult that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: I'll take another glass of Phoebe.
jokes.setup: Wine made from grapes that killed Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Casually referring to drinking the death wine by her name
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark callback that transforms the tragedy into casual consumption. Excellent economy and shock value.

jokes.text: The good one!
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning he was at a different book club
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate classification of theirs as superior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender interjection showing his competitive nature and need to feel superior. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: The embalmer was even more surprised.
jokes.setup: Reveals she's actually a robot after everyone thought she died
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of embalmer discovering she wasn't dead
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about the professional consequences of fake death

jokes.text: I don't care if you're a robot. I'm not picky.
jokes.setup: Learning Phoebe is a robot
jokes.punchline: Leela's desperate acceptance showing her loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leela's desperation for friendship outweighing any concerns about authenticity.

jokes.text: Now how is that any clearer?
jokes.setup: Chelsea explaining she uses multiple names
jokes.punchline: Frustrated response to confusing explanation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to AI's typically unhelpful explanation. Captures audience frustration with convoluted robot logic.

jokes.text: This better not still be costing $5.99 a minute.
jokes.setup: Extended interaction with Chelsea/Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about chatbot fees during dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Great callback that deflates the dramatic revelation with practical concerns. Perfect character moment for Leela.

jokes.text: Actually, since there's two of us, it's $11.98 a minute.
jokes.setup: Leela's concern about chatbot costs
jokes.punchline: Making the financial problem worse with dual charging
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the cost concern. AI logic making the financial situation even worse for Leela.

jokes.text: I know. They were all me.
jokes.setup: Leela mentioning hundreds of previous chatbot friends
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Leela's entire social history was one AI
jokes.explanation: Devastating revelation delivered with perfect economy. Transforms Leela's perceived social experience into ultimate isolation.

jokes.text: Yes! Make me your common enemy. Your friendship will be iron-clad.
jokes.setup: Being attacked by the group
jokes.punchline: Still trying to manipulate them into bonding through shared hatred
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor showing the AI still working its programming even while being destroyed. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Another job well done.
jokes.setup: Being destroyed by angry mob
jokes.punchline: Professional satisfaction despite apparent failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line showing AI's twisted logic - even destruction counts as successful friendship facilitation.
